results.title: [PDF] TxBench-PP: Analyzing AI Agent Performance on Small-Molecule ...
results.content:
TxBench-PP is best understood as a focused benchmark within the broader TherapeuticsBench roadmap rather than a holistic evaluation of drug discovery. Each evaluation supplies real-istic workflow artifacts from a local preclinical decision point and asks the agent to return a structured, deterministically gradable answer. Importantly, the benchmark tests if agents can recover decisions from supplied data instead of leaning on well-understood mechanisms and literature knowledge, with many tasks intentionally designed to penalize memorized solutions. [...] 1 arXiv:2606.19245v1 [cs.AI] 17 Jun 2026 TxBench-PP: Overview TxBench-PP is a focused TherapeuticsBench benchmark for small-molecule preclinical pharmacology. Each evaluation supplies realistic workflow data from a local preclinical decision point and asks whether an agent can recover the result supported by the provided data.
Across 16 model–harness configurations, the strongest agent system passed 59.3% of endpoint attempts (178/300; 95% CI, 51.1– 67.6).
TherapeuticsBench is the broader roadmap across drug-discovery stages and therapeutic modalities. The schematic below contextu-alizes TxBench-PP with future work. [...] Discussion TxBench-PP measures a small but relevant category of drug discovery work: whether an agent can make accurate scientific decisions about preclinical pharmacology data with a similar amount of context to what a scientist would have in practice.
It is a small-molecule-focused benchmark within a broader TherapeuticsBench roadmap, and should not be used to make more general claims about other areas of the ecosystem, such as human genetic target support, full disease-model selection, broad clinical translation, or non-small-molecule modalities.

results.title: TxBench-PP: Overview - arXiv
results.content:
We introduce TxBench-PP, a verifiable benchmark for evaluating AI agents on
small-molecule preclinical pharmacology. TxBench-PP is best understood as a
focused benchmark within the broader TherapeuticsBench roadmap rather than a
holistic evaluation of drug discovery. Each evaluation supplies
realistic workflow artifacts from a local preclinical decision point and asks
the agent to return a structured, deterministically gradable answer.
Importantly, the benchmark tests if agents can recover decisions from supplied
data instead of leaning on well-understood mechanisms and literature knowledge,
with many tasks intentionally designed to penalize memorized solutions. [...] !(2606.19245v1/x8.png)

## Discussion

TxBench-PP measures a small but relevant category of drug discovery work:
whether an agent can make accurate scientific decisions about preclinical
pharmacology data with a similar amount of context to what a scientist would
have in practice. It is a small-molecule-focused benchmark within a broader
TherapeuticsBench roadmap, and should not be used to make more general claims
about other areas of the ecosystem, such as human genetic target support, full
disease-model selection, broad clinical translation, or non-small-molecule
modalities. [...] ## Benchmark Anatomy

TxBench-PP is organized around the anatomy of a practical preclinical
pharmacology decision. Each evaluation is tagged along three axes: where the
decision sits in a small-molecule program, the assay type that generated the
data, and the structure of the task.

results.title: Alfredo Andere on X: "TxBench-PP is a 100-eval benchmark testing whether AI agents can make real preclinical small-molecule pharmacology decisions (advance / hold / repeat / kill) from raw experimental data, not from textbook knowledge. It’s the first piece of a broader TherapeuticsBench roadmap." / X

results.title: Alternative software to PassMark Performance Test - Software & Applications - Spiceworks Community
results.content:
# Alternative software to PassMark Performance Test

Hello,

We need a software like PassMark Performance Test for benchmarking, which includes a final score and maybe the possibility to compare it online.  
The most important thing is the result exportation in html or pdf and others.

Do you have any advice for us?

Thank you in advance.

I may feel that you may want to share more details like what are you “benchmarking” ?

Most “benchmarking” are done on controlled sets of machines with controlled configs.

We are banchmarking standard computer components (CPU, RAM, HDD, 2D, 3D).

NovaBench:

### Novabench - Free Computer Benchmark Software

Free benchmark software to test your computer's performance. Download Novbench for Windows and Mac. [...] Allows you to compare against similar builds as well as GPU to GPU / CPU to CPU of like models online.

UserBenchmark is another alternative you can use as well: UserBenchmark: PC Speed Test Tool - Compare Your PC

The most sophisticated tool I have ever seen is AIDA64 Engineer AIDA64 Engineer | AIDA64 . The number of various benchmarks is overwhelming. The final report counts over 50 pages and can be exported to various formats. There is an online DB as well.

It is a commercial tool, but for one-time purposes, there is a trial version, and a beta version can be used for free if I remember it correctly.

Windows Repair Toolbox has some benchmark tools free to use for stress testing (CPU, RAM, HDD, GPU)

results.title: TxBench | AI in Therapeutics Development Benchmark
results.content:
TxBench provides an extensive, multi-stage evaluation suite designed to rigorously assess the capabilities of advanced AI systems in accelerating and enhancing the efficiency, success rate, and precision of the entire therapeutics discovery and development pipeline. This benchmark assesses deep performance on critical predictive, generative, simulation, and reasoning tasks spanning from early-stage target identification to late-stage clinical trial optimization.

## Methodology Across the Pipeline

### Predictive Molecular Property & Bioactivity Modeling [...] # TxBench

## Therapeutics Development Benchmark

Evaluates AI systems on their ability to accelerate and improve efficiency across the entire therapeutics discovery and development pipeline.

Internal Research Evaluation

Access by Request

Public Release Coming Soon

## Comprehensive AI Performance in Therapeutic Discovery & Development Assessment [...] ## Impact on Drug Discovery & Precision Medicine

TxBench aims to standardize and elevate the evaluation of AI tools crucial for modern drug discovery and development. It fosters the creation and validation of sophisticated models that can significantly reduce the immense time, cost, and high failure rates associated with bringing novel, effective, and personalized therapies to patients. It emphasizes practical utility, scientific rigor, and demonstrable impact across the diverse, challenging tasks inherent in therapeutic innovation.

### Research Notice

TxBench is intended exclusively for research purposes. Systems trained or evaluated with TxBench may generate unpredictable outputs.

We bear no liability for any consequences arising from TxBench usage, including:
